main_paper.raw: {"methods":["Vec2Face: a holistic face synthesis model composed of a feature masked autoencoder and an image decoder, using sampled vectors as input to generate face images with controllable identity and attributes","Sampling of input vectors with low cosine similarity to ensure inter-class separation","Perturbation of input vectors within a small range to generate intra-class variation for each identity","Gradient descent-based adjustment of input vectors to generate images with designated attributes (e.g., pose, age, facial hair)"],"problems":["Synthesizing large-scale face datasets of non-existent persons for effective training of face recognition (FR) models","Achieving high inter-class separation (distinct identities) in synthetic face datasets","Achieving proper intra-class variation (diversity within each identity) in synthetic face datasets","Overcoming limitations of existing methods in controlling identity and attribute generation without external models","Addressing privacy issues associated with web-scraped real face datasets"],"datasets":["HSFace (synthetic dataset generated by Vec2Face, with up to 300K identities and 15 million images)","LFW (Labeled Faces in the Wild)","CFP-FP (Celebrities in Frontal-Profile)","AgeDB-30","CALFW (Cross-Age LFW)","CPLFW (Cross-Pose LFW)","IJBB (IARPA Janus Benchmark-B)","IJBC (IARPA Janus Benchmark-C)"],"metrics":["Face recognition accuracy (on various test sets)","Inter-class separability (measured via cosine similarity between identity vectors)","Intra-class variation (measured via similarity between perturbed vectors and original identity vectors)"],"results":[{"metric":"Face recognition accuracy (average on LFW, CFP-FP, AgeDB-30, CALFW, CPLFW)","value":"92% to 93.52% (using HSFace datasets from 10K to 300K identities)"},{"metric":"Face recognition accuracy (CALFW, IJBB, IJBC)","value":"Higher than models trained on same-scale real data (first time achieved with synthetic data)"},{"metric":"Number of synthetic identities generated","value":"Up to 300K (previous works limited to 60K)"},{"metric":"Number of synthetic images generated","value":"Up to 15 million"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes Vec2Face, a holistic model that can generate a large number of well-separated face identities and intra-class variations using only sampled vectors as input, without external attribute augmentation models.","Demonstrates for the first time that a synthetic face dataset can be scaled to 300K identities and 15 million images, surpassing previous works limited to 60K identities.","Shows that face recognition models trained on the synthetic dataset can outperform those trained on real data of the same scale on several benchmarks (CALFW, IJBB, IJBC).","Introduces a method to control both inter-class separability and intra-class variation directly via vector sampling and perturbation, and to generate images with designated attributes via gradient descent on input vectors."]}

methods: Vec2Face: a holistic face synthesis model composed of a feature masked autoencoder and an image decoder, using sampled vectors as input to generate face images with controllable identity and attributes
Sampling of input vectors with low cosine similarity to ensure inter-class separation
Perturbation of input vectors within a small range to generate intra-class variation for each identity
Gradient descent-based adjustment of input vectors to generate images with designated attributes (e.g., pose, age, facial hair)
problems: Synthesizing large-scale face datasets of non-existent persons for effective training of face recognition (FR) models
Achieving high inter-class separation (distinct identities) in synthetic face datasets
Achieving proper intra-class variation (diversity within each identity) in synthetic face datasets
Overcoming limitations of existing methods in controlling identity and attribute generation without external models
Addressing privacy issues associated with web-scraped real face datasets
datasets: HSFace (synthetic dataset generated by Vec2Face, with up to 300K identities and 15 million images)
metrics: Face recognition accuracy (on various test sets)
Inter-class separability (measured via cosine similarity between identity vectors)
Intra-class variation (measured via similarity between perturbed vectors and original identity vectors)

results.metric: Face recognition accuracy (average on LFW, CFP-FP, AgeDB-30, CALFW, CPLFW)
results.value: 92% to 93.52% (using HSFace datasets from 10K to 300K identities)
Higher than models trained on same-scale real data (first time achieved with synthetic data)
Up to 300K (previous works limited to 60K)
Up to 15 million

novelty_claims: Proposes Vec2Face, a holistic model that can generate a large number of well-separated face identities and intra-class variations using only sampled vectors as input, without external attribute augmentation models.
Demonstrates for the first time that a synthetic face dataset can be scaled to 300K identities and 15 million images, surpassing previous works limited to 60K identities.
Shows that face recognition models trained on the synthetic dataset can outperform those trained on real data of the same scale on several benchmarks (CALFW, IJBB, IJBC).

Introduces a method to control both inter-class separability and intra-class variation directly via vector sampling and perturbation, and to generate images with designated attributes via gradient descent on input vectors.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["SynFace: face recognition using synthetic face images","Identity Mixup (IM): enlarging intra-class variations in synthetic face images by mixing identity coefficients during generation","Domain Mixup (DM): combining synthetic and real face images during training to reduce domain gap","Systematic empirical analysis of synthetic dataset properties (depth, width, facial attributes)"],"problems":["Difficulty in collecting large-scale real-world face recognition data due to label noise and privacy issues","Lack of detailed attribute annotations (pose, expression, etc.) in real datasets, limiting analysis of attribute influence","Performance gap between models trained on synthetic vs. real face images due to poor intra-class variation and domain gap","Long-tailed distribution in real datasets (many classes with few samples)"],"datasets":["CASIA-WebFace (real)","Synthetic face images generated by DiscoFaceGAN with identity mixup","Other real and synthetic datasets for cross-domain evaluation (not all named explicitly)"],"metrics":["Face recognition accuracy (verification and identification rates)","Cross-domain evaluation accuracy"],"results":[{"metric":"Performance gap (accuracy) between real-trained and synthetic-trained models","value":"Significantly reduced with identity mixup and domain mixup (exact numbers not provided in excerpt)"},{"metric":"Effect of dataset width vs. depth","value":"Width (number of identities) is more important than depth (samples per identity) for recognition performance"}],"novelty_claims":["First systematic exploration of face recognition using fully synthetic data with controllable attributes","Introduction of identity mixup (IM) to increase intra-class variation in synthetic face images for improved recognition","Introduction of domain mixup (DM) to bridge the domain gap between synthetic and real face images","Empirical analysis revealing the importance of dataset width over depth for synthetic face recognition","Analysis of the influence of facial attributes (pose, expression, illumination) on recognition using synthetic data"]}

methods: SynFace: face recognition using synthetic face images
Identity Mixup (IM): enlarging intra-class variations in synthetic face images by mixing identity coefficients during generation
Domain Mixup (DM): combining synthetic and real face images during training to reduce domain gap
Systematic empirical analysis of synthetic dataset properties (depth, width, facial attributes)
problems: Difficulty in collecting large-scale real-world face recognition data due to label noise and privacy issues
Lack of detailed attribute annotations (pose, expression, etc.) in real datasets, limiting analysis of attribute influence
Performance gap between models trained on synthetic vs. real face images due to poor intra-class variation and domain gap
Long-tailed distribution in real datasets (many classes with few samples)
datasets: Synthetic face images generated by DiscoFaceGAN with identity mixup
Other real and synthetic datasets for cross-domain evaluation (not all named explicitly)
metrics: Face recognition accuracy (verification and identification rates)

results.metric: Performance gap (accuracy) between real-trained and synthetic-trained models
results.value: Significantly reduced with identity mixup and domain mixup (exact numbers not provided in excerpt)

results.metric: Effect of dataset width vs. depth
results.value: Width (number of identities) is more important than depth (samples per identity) for recognition performance

novelty_claims: First systematic exploration of face recognition using fully synthetic data with controllable attributes
Introduction of identity mixup (IM) to increase intra-class variation in synthetic face images for improved recognition
Introduction of domain mixup (DM) to bridge the domain gap between synthetic and real face images
Empirical analysis revealing the importance of dataset width over depth for synthetic face recognition
Analysis of the influence of facial attributes (pose, expression, illumination) on recognition using synthetic data

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Dual Condition Face Generator (DCFace) based on a diffusion model","Patch-wise style extractor","Time-step dependent ID loss"],"problems":["Generating synthetic face datasets for training face recognition models with high fidelity and controlled intra-class and inter-class variations","Consistently generating multiple images of the same subject under different styles while preserving identity","Addressing privacy, label noise, and demographic bias issues in real face datasets"],"datasets":["LFW","CFP-FP","CPLFW","AgeDB","CALFW"],"metrics":["Verification accuracy"],"results":[{"metric":"Verification accuracy (average improvement over previous works)","value":"6.11% higher on average in 4 out of 5 test datasets"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes a dual condition diffusion model (DCFace) that combines subject appearance (ID) and external factor (style) conditions for synthetic face generation","Introduces a novel patch-wise style extractor for precise style control","Introduces a time-step dependent ID loss to ensure consistent identity preservation across generated images","Demonstrates that face recognition models trained on DCFace synthetic images outperform previous synthetic data generation methods in verification accuracy"]}

methods: Dual Condition Face Generator (DCFace) based on a diffusion model
Patch-wise style extractor
Time-step dependent ID loss
problems: Generating synthetic face datasets for training face recognition models with high fidelity and controlled intra-class and inter-class variations
Consistently generating multiple images of the same subject under different styles while preserving identity
Addressing privacy, label noise, and demographic bias issues in real face datasets

results.metric: Verification accuracy (average improvement over previous works)
results.value: 6.11% higher on average in 4 out of 5 test datasets

novelty_claims: Proposes a dual condition diffusion model (DCFace) that combines subject appearance (ID) and external factor (style) conditions for synthetic face generation
Introduces a novel patch-wise style extractor for precise style control
Introduces a time-step dependent ID loss to ensure consistent identity preservation across generated images
Demonstrates that face recognition models trained on DCFace synthetic images outperform previous synthetic data generation methods in verification accuracy

methods: Creation of a large-scale synthetic face dataset (DigiFace-1M) using a computer graphics pipeline for rendering digital faces with controlled variation in attributes (pose, accessories, textures, etc.)
Aggressive data augmentation to reduce the synthetic-to-real domain gap
Fine-tuning face recognition models trained on synthetic data with a small number of real face images collected with consent
Systematic study of the effect of different facial attributes on recognition accuracy
problems: Ethical issues in large-scale face recognition datasets due to privacy violations and lack of consent
Label noise in web-crawled face datasets
Data bias in existing face datasets (e.g., racial imbalance, lighting, makeup)
Domain gap between synthetic and real face images in face recognition
datasets: Five popular face recognition benchmarks (not all named, but LFW is one of them)

Average accuracy on five benchmarks after fine-tuning with 120K real images

novelty_claims: Release of the largest public synthetic dataset for face recognition (1.22M images, 110K identities) that is free from privacy violations and label noise
Demonstration that synthetic data, when combined with aggressive augmentation and a small amount of real data, can achieve accuracy comparable to models trained on millions of real images
Systematic study of how control over rendering pipeline attributes (pose, accessories, etc.) affects recognition accuracy
Significant reduction in error rates compared to previous synthetic face datasets (e.g., SynFace)

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Three-player Generative Adversarial Network (IDnet) with an identity-dedicated third player (ID-3)","Extension of class-conditioned GANs to enforce identity-discriminant information during generation"],"problems":["Lack of privacy and legal/ethical concerns in using real face datasets for face recognition","Low identity separability and intra-class variation in synthetic face datasets generated by existing GAN-based methods","Need for synthetic face datasets that enable effective training of face recognition models without using real identities"],"datasets":["SFace synthetic dataset","DigiFace-1M","SynFace","USynthFace","IDnet synthetic dataset (proposed)"],"metrics":["Face recognition accuracy","Identity separability","Intra-class variance","Similarity between synthetic and authentic identities"],"results":[{"metric":"Face recognition accuracy (IDnet vs SFace and other GAN-based datasets)","value":"IDnet outperforms SFace and all existing GAN-based approaches; achieves comparable results to DigiFace-1M (rendering-based approach)"},{"metric":"Identity similarity between synthetic and authentic identities","value":"Very low similarities, indicating privacy preservation"},{"metric":"Identity discrimination (separability)","value":"IDnet synthetic images have higher identity discrimination than conventional two-player GANs (e.g., SFace)"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes a three-player GAN (IDnet) with an identity-dedicated third player to enforce identity-discriminant information in synthetic face generation","Demonstrates that IDnet generates synthetic identities with higher class separability and realistic intra-class variation compared to previous GAN-based methods","Shows that face recognition models trained on IDnet synthetic data outperform those trained on other GAN-based synthetic datasets and are comparable to rendering-based approaches, while being less computationally demanding","Empirically proves low similarity between synthetic and authentic identities, addressing privacy concerns"]}

methods: Three-player Generative Adversarial Network (IDnet) with an identity-dedicated third player (ID-3)
Extension of class-conditioned GANs to enforce identity-discriminant information during generation
problems: Lack of privacy and legal/ethical concerns in using real face datasets for face recognition
Low identity separability and intra-class variation in synthetic face datasets generated by existing GAN-based methods
Need for synthetic face datasets that enable effective training of face recognition models without using real identities
metrics: Similarity between synthetic and authentic identities

results.metric: Face recognition accuracy (IDnet vs SFace and other GAN-based datasets)
results.value: IDnet outperforms SFace and all existing GAN-based approaches; achieves comparable results to DigiFace-1M (rendering-based approach)

results.metric: Identity similarity between synthetic and authentic identities
results.value: Very low similarities, indicating privacy preservation
IDnet synthetic images have higher identity discrimination than conventional two-player GANs (e.g., SFace)

novelty_claims: Proposes a three-player GAN (IDnet) with an identity-dedicated third player to enforce identity-discriminant information in synthetic face generation
Demonstrates that IDnet generates synthetic identities with higher class separability and realistic intra-class variation compared to previous GAN-based methods

Shows that face recognition models trained on IDnet synthetic data outperform those trained on other GAN-based synthetic datasets and are comparable to rendering-based approaches, while being less computationally demanding
Empirically proves low similarity between synthetic and authentic identities, addressing privacy concerns

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["ExFaceGAN: a framework to disentangle identity information in pretrained GANs' latent spaces by learning an identity directional boundary, splitting the latent space into identity-similar and identity-dissimilar subspaces, enabling generation of multiple samples per synthetic identity without attribute supervision or architecture changes","Integration of ExFaceGAN into learned latent spaces of three state-of-the-art GAN approaches","A sampling mechanism to control inter-class variation and intra-class compactness in generated data"],"problems":["Difficulty in generating multiple samples of a specific synthetic identity with realistic intra-class variations using GANs, due to entangled attributes in latent space","Existing methods require additional supervision, attribute classifiers, or dedicated architectures to control identity and attributes, limiting diversity and scalability","Need for synthetic data with high identity discrimination for training face recognition models, without relying on real biometric data"],"datasets":[],"metrics":["Identity discrimination (e.g., face recognition accuracy)","Diversity of generated samples (intra-class variation, inter-class variation)"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["Proposes ExFaceGAN, a novel framework to discover and disentangle identity directions in the latent space of pretrained GANs, enabling generation of multiple samples per synthetic identity with realistic intra-class variation","Does not require human labeling, attribute classifiers, or changes to GAN architecture for identity disentanglement","Can turn any pretrained unconditional GAN into an identity-conditional GAN without retraining","Demonstrates that ExFaceGAN-generated synthetic data can be used to train face recognition models that outperform all previous GAN-based face recognition models","Provides a sampling mechanism to control inter-class variation and intra-class compactness in generated data"]}

methods: ExFaceGAN: a framework to disentangle identity information in pretrained GANs' latent spaces by learning an identity directional boundary, splitting the latent space into identity-similar and identity-dissimilar subspaces, enabling generation of multiple samples per synthetic identity without attribute supervision or architecture changes
Integration of ExFaceGAN into learned latent spaces of three state-of-the-art GAN approaches
A sampling mechanism to control inter-class variation and intra-class compactness in generated data
problems: Difficulty in generating multiple samples of a specific synthetic identity with realistic intra-class variations using GANs, due to entangled attributes in latent space
Existing methods require additional supervision, attribute classifiers, or dedicated architectures to control identity and attributes, limiting diversity and scalability
Need for synthetic data with high identity discrimination for training face recognition models, without relying on real biometric data
metrics: Diversity of generated samples (intra-class variation, inter-class variation)
novelty_claims: Proposes ExFaceGAN, a novel framework to discover and disentangle identity directions in the latent space of pretrained GANs, enabling generation of multiple samples per synthetic identity with realistic intra-class variation
Does not require human labeling, attribute classifiers, or changes to GAN architecture for identity disentanglement
Can turn any pretrained unconditional GAN into an identity-conditional GAN without retraining
Demonstrates that ExFaceGAN-generated synthetic data can be used to train face recognition models that outperform all previous GAN-based face recognition models
Provides a sampling mechanism to control inter-class variation and intra-class compactness in generated data

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Class-conditional generative adversarial network (GAN) to generate synthetic face images (SFace)","Three learning strategies for training face recognition (FR) models on synthetic data: (1) Multi-class classification using CosFace loss, (2) Label-free knowledge transfer (KT) from a pre-trained model, (3) Combined learning of multi-class classification and knowledge transfer"],"problems":["Lack of privacy-friendly, shareable face datasets for training face recognition models due to legal and ethical concerns","Feasibility of using synthetic data for training accurate face recognition models without privacy risks","Ensuring that synthetic data does not leak identity information from the original authentic datasets"],"datasets":["SFace (synthetic dataset generated in this work)","LFW (Labeled Faces in the Wild)","Four other authentic face benchmarks (not named explicitly in the provided text)"],"metrics":["Verification accuracy"],"results":[{"metric":"Verification accuracy on LFW (multi-class classification)","value":"91.87%"},{"metric":"Verification accuracy on LFW (combined learning strategy)","value":"99.13%"}],"novelty_claims":["First to demonstrate the feasibility of training accurate face recognition models using only privacy-friendly synthetic data generated by a class-conditional GAN.","Propose and analyze three distinct learning strategies for training FR models on synthetic data: multi-class classification, label-free knowledge transfer, and their combination.","Provide extensive evaluation of the identity relation between synthetic and authentic datasets, showing that associating identities between them is hardly possible, thus ensuring privacy.","Analyze the effect of synthetic data size (samples per identity) on FR model performance under different learning strategies.","Publicly release the synthetic dataset (SFace) and training code for reproducibility."]}

methods: Class-conditional generative adversarial network (GAN) to generate synthetic face images (SFace)

Three learning strategies for training face recognition (FR) models on synthetic data: (1) Multi-class classification using CosFace loss, (2) Label-free knowledge transfer (KT) from a pre-trained model, (3) Combined learning of multi-class classification and knowledge transfer
problems: Lack of privacy-friendly, shareable face datasets for training face recognition models due to legal and ethical concerns
Feasibility of using synthetic data for training accurate face recognition models without privacy risks
Ensuring that synthetic data does not leak identity information from the original authentic datasets
datasets: SFace (synthetic dataset generated in this work)
Four other authentic face benchmarks (not named explicitly in the provided text)

results.metric: Verification accuracy on LFW (combined learning strategy)

novelty_claims: First to demonstrate the feasibility of training accurate face recognition models using only privacy-friendly synthetic data generated by a class-conditional GAN.
Propose and analyze three distinct learning strategies for training FR models on synthetic data: multi-class classification, label-free knowledge transfer, and their combination.
Provide extensive evaluation of the identity relation between synthetic and authentic datasets, showing that associating identities between them is hardly possible, thus ensuring privacy.
Analyze the effect of synthetic data size (samples per identity) on FR model performance under different learning strategies.
Publicly release the synthetic dataset (SFace) and training code for reproducibility.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["ID3: Identity-Preserving-yet-Diversified Diffusion Model for SFR","ID-preserving loss function for diffusion models","ID-preserving sampling algorithm based on adjusted gradient vector field","Dataset-generating algorithm using Tammes problem for inter-class diversity and random perturbation for intra-class diversity"],"problems":["Generating synthetic face datasets that mimic real face data for privacy-preserving face recognition","Ensuring inter-class diversity (many distinct identities) in synthetic datasets","Ensuring intra-class diversity (varied facial attributes within each identity)","Maintaining intra-class identity preservation (identity consistency within each class)","Improving generalization of synthetic face recognition models to real-world faces"],"datasets":["Five challenging face recognition benchmarks (specific names not provided in the excerpt)"],"metrics":["Verification accuracy"],"results":[{"metric":"Verification accuracy improvement over SoTA SFR approaches (average across five benchmarks)","value":"~2.4%"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes ID3, a diffusion-based generative model that jointly enforces identity preservation and diversity for synthetic face recognition datasets.","Introduces a new loss function for diffusion models that explicitly preserves identity and is theoretically equivalent to maximizing a lower bound of an adjusted conditional log-likelihood.","Designs a novel ID-preserving sampling algorithm that operates over an adjusted gradient vector field for generating synthetic faces.","Presents a dataset-generating algorithm that ensures inter-class diversity via the Tammes problem and intra-class diversity via random perturbations of identity embeddings.","Demonstrates superior performance compared to state-of-the-art SFR methods across multiple benchmarks."]}

methods: ID3: Identity-Preserving-yet-Diversified Diffusion Model for SFR
ID-preserving loss function for diffusion models
ID-preserving sampling algorithm based on adjusted gradient vector field
Dataset-generating algorithm using Tammes problem for inter-class diversity and random perturbation for intra-class diversity
problems: Generating synthetic face datasets that mimic real face data for privacy-preserving face recognition
Ensuring inter-class diversity (many distinct identities) in synthetic datasets
Ensuring intra-class diversity (varied facial attributes within each identity)
Maintaining intra-class identity preservation (identity consistency within each class)
Improving generalization of synthetic face recognition models to real-world faces
datasets: Five challenging face recognition benchmarks (specific names not provided in the excerpt)

results.metric: Verification accuracy improvement over SoTA SFR approaches (average across five benchmarks)

novelty_claims: Proposes ID3, a diffusion-based generative model that jointly enforces identity preservation and diversity for synthetic face recognition datasets.
Introduces a new loss function for diffusion models that explicitly preserves identity and is theoretically equivalent to maximizing a lower bound of an adjusted conditional log-likelihood.
Designs a novel ID-preserving sampling algorithm that operates over an adjusted gradient vector field for generating synthetic faces.
Presents a dataset-generating algorithm that ensures inter-class diversity via the Tammes problem and intra-class diversity via random perturbations of identity embeddings.
Demonstrates superior performance compared to state-of-the-art SFR methods across multiple benchmarks.
